rulings.Case Title: PRIYA SANGER et al VS. LEAH AHN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To: (1) Fix Amount Owed Pursuant To Arbitration Award, (2) Conirm Payment Of Award Under Protest, (3) Suspend Writ Of Execurtion, (4) Release Liens, And (5) For Such Other And Further Relief As The Court Deems Just And Equitable
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, line 1. DEFENDANT LEAH AHNS Motion To: (1) Fix Amount Owed Pursuant To Arbitration Award, (2) Conirm Payment Of Award Under Protest, (3) Suspend Writ Of Execurtion, (4) Release Liens, And (5) For Such Other And Further Relief As The Court Deems Just And Equitable is continued to June 29, 2015 per June 15, 2015 ex-parte order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: HANIEL OLIVERA et al VS. KAUSHIK DATTANI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Defendants Motion For Costs, Including Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, line 2. DEFENDANT KAUSHIK DATTANI and 363 VALENCIA'S Motion And Defendants Motion For Costs, Including Attorneys Fees is continued to August 12, 2015. Moving party to provide supplemental declaration with unredacted invoices no later than July 16, 2015. (Attorney-client and work product privileged information may be redacted. Notations such as "evaluated complaint, "researched motion to compel" and "attended the hearing" are not privileged). Plaintiffs may file an opposition limited to the supplemental declaration, not to exceed 5 pages, no later than July 30, 2015. Moving party may file a reply, not to exceed 5 pages, no later than August 5, 2015. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ZOILA ACOSTA MURCIA ET AL VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, line 3. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE, RECONTRUST COMPANY, and HSBC DEUTCHE BANK N.A. AS TRUSTEE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurer is sustained with leave to amend as to fraud cause of action to allege each element with requisite specificity. Demurrer is otherwise overruled, causes of action for breach of contract, negligence and HBOR violations are sufficiently alleged. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KEYHAN MOHANNA VS. COUNTRYWIDE BANK, A DIVISION OF TREASURY BANK,N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Pursuant To California Civil Code Section 473(B)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA'S Motion To Set Aside Default Pursuant To California Civil Code Section 473(B) is GRANTED, no opposition filed. Answer shall be filed and served forthwith. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE VS. KIMBERLY CLARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositin Of Deft And Request For Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, line 7. PLAINTIFF BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE'S Motion To Compel Depositin Of Defendant And Request For Monetary Sanction is GRANTED. Defendant shall appear for deposition. Sanctions granted in the amount of $200. No indication of what the "total amount incurred by Plaintiff for filing this motion" is representing is provided in the moving papers. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE VS. KIMBERLY CLARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Ofmotion And Motion To Copel Enforcement Of Order For Monetary Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 24, 2015, line 7. PLAINTIFF BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE'S Motion To Copel Enforcement Of Order For Monetary Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED. Defendant complied with discovery order by providing responses. In regards to monetary sanctions, Plaintiff has remedies available to it for collection. = (501/REQ)
